<!-- Generated by the rsc-client-milestone1-numbers workflow, 2026-06-01. -->

# RSC 3-Way Render Diff — design

## 0. Goal and the two questions

We want to render the **same world/scene state** through three independent renderers and pixel-diff the outputs, to prove (1) our Go `render/` lib and (2) the deobfuscated Java client are faithful to (3) the vanilla rev-235 jar, and to localize perception bugs (unwalkable door, broken bridge, roof culling).

The three engines:

1. **JAR** — the obfuscated rev-235 `rsclassic-1091943135.jar` driven by **rscplus** (ASM-wrapped, reflection on obf names). This is the **oracle / ground truth**.
2. **DEOB** — the deobfuscated Java in `reference/rsc-client/src/client` (`world.World` = obf `k`, `scene.Scene` = obf `lb`, `scene.GameModel` = obf `ca`, `scene.Surface` = obf `ua`).
3. **GO** — `westworld/render` consuming a `render.View` against a `pathfind.Landscape` + `facts.Facts` + model `Bundle`.

**Answering the two explicit questions up front:**

**(a) How do we set up the scenes with EXACTLY the same state across all 3?**
By making **state a serialized artifact, not a live session**. We do NOT try to log all three clients into a live server and hope they converge — RNG (`Math.random()` terrain speckle in `World.buildSection`, network timing, animation frame) guarantees divergence. Instead one engine **dumps** a canonical state blob at a chosen tick, and all three **load** that identical blob. The JAR is the natural authoritative source (it owns the real map decode + camera), with rscplus **replays** as the deterministic driver. See §3, §4.

**(b) Can we build a tool that DUMPS state from the client and replay it across all 3?**
Yes — and it is the keystone of this whole design. Two dump *levels* (§1). The Go `render.View` is **already a partial L1 dump format** (`buildLiveView` in `cmd/cradle/spectate.go` literally snapshots "everything the host perceives" into a serializable struct). We formalize that into a versioned schema (§4) that all three can produce and consume, and add a jar-side hook (§3) to emit it. The L2 (low-level GameModel) dump is a second, narrower tool to isolate the rasterizer.

---

## 1. The two dump levels, and which to do first

A frame is determined by: **a camera** + **a list of `GameModel`s (vertices/faces/fills/intensities)** that the renderer projects and rasterizes. The `GameModel` list is in turn *built* from decoded world state by `World.buildSection`. That gives us two natural cut points:

### L1 — high-level WORLD state (RECOMMENDED FIRST)
The decoded server/map state at a tick, *before* mesh building:
- Terrain grid: per-tile `GroundElevation`, `GroundTexture` (colour), `GroundOverlay` (decoration), `RoofTexture`, `HorizontalWall`/`VerticalWall`/`DiagonalWalls` ids — i.e. exactly the per-quadrant grids `World` decodes in `loadMapData` (`L/eb/f/P/s/A/R/mb`).
- Dynamic state: scenery locs, npcs, players, ground items, removed-boundary/removed-scenery overrides, local-player pos + heading + appearance.
- Camera params.

**Each engine builds its OWN scene from this**, so an L1 diff exercises the *scene-builder*:
- `World.buildSection`/`buildRoofs`/`method422`/`addModels` in DEOB,
- `BuildTerrain`/`BuildBoundaries`/`BuildRoofs`/`PlaceScenery` in GO,
- the jar's obf `k.a(...)` mesher.

**The three named bugs all live in World→GameModel building, so L1 is the primary target:**
- **Unwalkable door** — `World.addModels` (diagonal door objects, id 48001..59999) + `setWallObjectAdjacency`/`removeObject2` passability, and `BuildBoundaries`' `removed` override path. The Go side already has authentic-vs-our divergence notes here (`render.View.BoundaryRemoved`, boundary.go).
- **Broken bridge** — the overlay-250 remap. DEOB does it in `setTiles`/`getTileTypeOnPlane`/`buildOverlayTriangles`; GO does it in `terrain.go` (`bridgeOverlay`, `bridgeOrWater`, the `wx%48==47` chunk-seam test). Mismatch here = the "all messed up" bridge.
- **Roof culling** — `HostUnderRoof`/`!underRoof` gating in GO `render.go` vs DEOB's `getWallRoof`/`isRoofCorner`/`hasRoof` + the under-roof wall/roof suppression. Plus the roof-ridge `buildRoofs` seven-way cascade.

### L2 — low-level SCENE state (isolation layer)
The exact built `GameModel[]` list + camera, *after* mesh building — every model's `numVertices`, base `vertexX/Y/Z`, `numFaces`, `faceVertices[]`, `faceFillFront/Back`, `faceIntensity`, plus per-model transform (`baseX/Y/Z`, orientation) and `lightAmbience/Diffuse/Direction`.

An L2 diff feeds **identical geometry** to all three rasterizers, so it **isolates the rasterizer/projector** (`Scene.render`/`GameModel.project` ↔ `Scene.RenderTo`/`rasterFace`/`method281`/`method291`/`method282`). If L1 differs but L2 (built from the JAR's own models) matches, the bug is in *our* scene-builder, not the rasterizer — and vice-versa. **L2 is the bisection tool**, built second.

**Recommendation:** Build **L1 first** (it targets the actual bugs and reuses the existing `render.View`), then **L2** as the rasterizer-isolation layer once L1 diffs reveal whether a discrepancy is build-side or raster-side.

---

## 2. Per-engine render inputs (what each consumes today)

**DEOB Java** (`scene.Scene`):
- Camera set via `Scene.setCameraOrientation(camX, camY, eyeLen, yaw, mode=-12349, pitch, eyeY, roll)` → stores inverse angles `cameraYaw/Pitch/Roll` (obf `b/Kb/xb`) and look offset `cameraLookX/Y/Z` (obf `bc/o/I`); `setBounds(clipY, _, width, clipX, baseY, viewDistance, baseX)`.
- Models: `Scene.addModel(GameModel)` (obf `Z[]`), each carrying base verts (`vertexX/Y/Z` = `a/ob/bc`), faces (`faceVertices`=`o`, `faceFillFront/Back`=`V/qb`, `faceIntensity`=`Hb`), lighting (`lightAmbience/Diffuse`=`Jb/Mb`, dir `g/Bb/Fb`).
- `World` builds them: terrain into `parentModel` then `split` into `terrainModels[64]`; walls via `method422` into `wallModels[plane][64]`; roofs via `buildRoofs` into `roofModels[plane][64]`; diagonal doors via `addModels`.
- Output: `Surface.pixels` int[] (obf `ua.rb`), `Surface.width/height` (obf `u/k`).

**GO** (`render.View` → `Scene` → `Surface`):
- `View{X,Y,Plane,Rotation,Zoom,W,H, Entities[], DynamicScenery[], GroundItems[], BoundaryRemoved fn, SceneryRemoved fn, NoSelf, Self*}`.
- `RenderView` builds `BuildTerrain` + `BuildBoundaries` + `BuildRoofs` + `PlaceScenery` into `Scene.Models`, sets `Scene.Cam = SetCamera(localX, -elev, localZ, 912, rotation*4, 0, zoom*2)`, then `RenderTo(surf, W/2, H/2)`.
- World data comes from `pathfind.Landscape.Tile(x,y,plane)` → `Tile{GroundElevation, GroundTexture, GroundOverlay, RoofTexture, HorizontalWall, VerticalWall, DiagonalWalls}` and `facts.Facts` (scenery defs/locs).
- Output: `Surface.Pix` []int32 → `Surface.PNG()`.

**The `render.View` is already an L1 dump format.** The shared schema is essentially `View` + the *terrain grid itself* (which GO currently reads lazily from `Landscape`, but a dump must carry explicitly so the JAR/DEOB feed the same bytes rather than re-decoding map files).

---

## 3. JAR / rscplus dump — where to hook the vanilla client

rscplus already does exactly the mirroring we need, verified in `src/Client/JClassPatcher.java`:
```
hookClassVariable(methodNode, "ua","rb","[I","Game/Renderer","pixels","[I", true,true);  // Surface pixel buf
hookClassVariable(methodNode, "ua","u","I","Game/Renderer","width","I", false,true);
hookClassVariable(methodNode, "ua","k","I","Game/Renderer","height","I", false,true);
```
So `Renderer.pixels` IS the obfuscated `Surface` framebuffer. Camera state is already surfaced in `Game/Camera.java` (`rotation`, `zoom`, `fov`, `lookat_x/y`, `distance1..4`, `pitch_internal`). This proves the hook surface is reachable.

**Two dump strategies (use both, for the two levels):**

**3a. L1 dump via a state-serialization hook (PRIMARY).**
Add a small `StateDumper` class in rscplus and a `hookClassVariable`/method-entry hook so that at a chosen tick we read:
- World grids off obf `k` (World): the per-quadrant `byte[][]` grids `L/eb/f/P/s/A/R/mb` and convert to the absolute-tile schema grids. (These are package-private instance fields; rscplus reaches obf members by name via its existing ASM hook machinery — same mechanism as the `ua.rb` hook above, just reading instead of mirroring.)
- Camera off `Camera.*` (already mirrored) — `rotation`, `zoom`, `lookat_x/y`, `pitch_internal`, plus the fixed `fov`/distance.
- Entities/scenery/items off the main `client` (Mudclient) actor arrays + local player pos.

Serialize to the canonical JSON (§4). Feasibility: **High** — the grids are plain `byte[][]`, the hook mechanism is proven, no obf-method execution needed.

> Caveat from prior findings: the obf primitive classes still reference the always-false opaque predicate `client.vh`; **do NOT try to execute isolated obf methods reflectively** to build geometry. The L1 dump only *reads decoded fields* — it never calls obf builders — so `client.vh` is irrelevant. (It would only bite an L2-from-jar dump if we tried to re-run the obf mesher in isolation, which we don't: for L2-from-jar we read the *already-built* `lb.Z[]` model array after the live client built it.)

**3b. Reuse rscplus REPLAYS as the deterministic state SOURCE (the driver).**
`Replay.java` has `frame_time_slice`, `timestamp`, replay seek machinery — a replay deterministically reproduces a recorded session. This solves determinism for the *jar* engine: seek to frame N, and the world/camera/entity state at that frame is fixed and reproducible across runs. The L1 hook then dumps at "frame N". Feasibility: **High** — replays already exist and are the rscplus headline feature; "render frame N → dump → diff" is a natural extension. **This is the recommended state source for non-hand-authored (real-map) diffs.**

**3c. L2 dump from jar (rasterizer isolation).**
After the live/replay client builds its scene, read obf `lb` (Scene) fields: `Z[]` (models, `ab` count), and for each `ca` model the base vertex/face arrays. Dump as the L2 schema. Feasibility: **Medium** — more obf fields to walk, but all are plain arrays; still no obf-method execution.

---

## 4. Determinism plan + canonical dump SCHEMA

### Determinism rules
1. **State is a file, not a session.** All three load the identical blob. The only engine that *decodes map files / runs the network loop* is the jar (the source); DEOB and GO consume the dumped grids directly and MUST skip their own map-file decode for diff runs.
2. **Kill RNG.** `World.buildSection` injects `(int)(Math.random()*10)-5` per terrain vertex as ambience speckle (`terrain.a(vid, amb, -61)`); GO's `BuildTerrain` and DEOB must use a **fixed ambience seed** (e.g. all-zero, or a deterministic PRNG seeded from a schema field `terrainSeed`) for diff builds. The Go side reads `vertexAmbience` per-vertex in `scene.go` rasterFace — that must be the same deterministic table.
3. **Pin animation.** `AnimFrame = 0` (frame 1) for static diffs; the schema carries `animFrame` so live/replay diffs can pin it.
4. **Pin window + camera exactly.** Same `baseX/baseY` window origin, same `W/H`, same `viewDist`, `clipNear`, `clipFar`, same screen centre `(W/2, H/2)`. NOTE a known constant drift to reconcile as part of this work: GO uses `viewDist=9, clipFar=7000, terrainSize=160`; DEOB `Scene` defaults `viewDistance=8` (overwritten by `setBounds`), the jar sets `clipFar_3d/2d=2400` per frame. **The schema pins these so the diff fails loudly if an engine uses a different constant** — that itself is a faithfulness bug to find.
5. **Same fill/colour tables.** Terrain colour ramps (`World` ctor builds 256-entry `terrainColours` via `ClientStream.rgb`), wall front/back colour tables, roof texture/ridge tables — these are GameData tables. The schema references them by id; each engine must resolve ids through the *same* GameData. For hand-authored scenes (§6) we sidestep this by emitting raw colours.

### Canonical schema (`rscdump` v1, JSON; binary L2 variant for big model lists)

```jsonc
{
  "schema": "rscdump/1",
  "level": "L1",                 // or "L2"
  "source": "jar-replay|deob|go",// who produced it (provenance)
  "tick": 12345,                 // replay frame / sim tick
  "camera": {
    "x": 6144, "y": -384, "z": 6144,   // eye position, world units (DEOB look offset I/o/bc; GO Camera.CameraX/Y/Z)
    "pitch": 912, "yaw": 504, "roll": 0,// 0..1023 camera angles (pre-inversion)
    "distance": 1500,                   // zoom*2
    "viewDist": 9, "clipNear": 5, "clipFar": 2400,
    "baseX": 256, "baseY": 256, "width": 512,
    "screenW": 512, "screenH": 334
  },
  "window": { "baseX": 200, "baseY": 540, "plane": 0, "size": 160 },
  "terrain": {                   // L1 ONLY — per-tile, absolute or window-local
    "size": 160,
    "elevation": [ ... ],        // size*size bytes
    "groundColour": [ ... ],     // size*size  (GroundTexture)
    "overlay": [ ... ],          // size*size  (GroundOverlay / decoration id; pre-remap)
    "roof": [ ... ],             // size*size  (RoofTexture / wall-roof id)
    "wallH": [ ... ],            // size*size  HorizontalWall (E/W)
    "wallV": [ ... ],            // size*size  VerticalWall  (N/S)
    "wallDiag": [ ... ],         // size*size  DiagonalWalls (int)
    "terrainSeed": 0             // deterministic ambience seed (RNG kill)
  },
  "scenery":   [ {"x":210,"y":545,"id":4,"dir":0}, ... ],
  "entities":  [ {"x":211,"y":546,"kind":"npc|player","id":3,"heading":2,
                  "equip":[...12],"hairCol":0,"topCol":0,"trouserCol":0,"skinCol":0} ],
  "groundItems":[ {"x":210,"y":545,"itemId":10} ],
  "self": {"x":208,"y":544,"heading":0,"hasEquip":true,"equip":[...12],"hair":..,"top":..,"trouser":..,"skin":..},
  "removedBoundaries": [ {"x":210,"y":545,"dir":0}, ... ],   // door-open overrides
  "removedScenery":    [ {"x":210,"y":545}, ... ],

  // L2 ONLY — built geometry (binary blob recommended; JSON shown for clarity)
  "models": [
    { "id": 0, "transparent": false, "depth": 0,
      "lightAmbience": 64, "lightDiffuse": 512, "lightDir": [180,155,95],
      "verts": [ [x,y,z], ... ],                 // base vertexX/Y/Z
      "vertexAmbience": [ ... ],                  // per-vertex speckle (RNG-pinned)
      "faces": [ {"v":[0,1,2,3], "fillFront":-321, "fillBack":12345678,
                  "intensity":0, "gouraud":false, "tag":200012} ]
    }, ...
  ]
}
```

### Schema-field → per-engine mapping table

| Schema field | DEOB source (file:member) | GO render field | JAR obf source (read by rscplus hook) |
|---|---|---|---|
| `camera.x/y/z` | `Scene` look offset `cameraLookX/Y/Z` (obf `bc/o/I`), set in `setCameraOrientation` | `Camera.CameraX/Y/Z` (`camera.go` `SetCamera`) | derived from `Camera.lookat_x/y` + `distance` (Camera.java) |
| `camera.pitch/yaw/roll` | `setCameraOrientation` args → `cameraYaw/Pitch/Roll` (obf `b/Kb/xb`) | `SetCamera(...,pitch,yaw,roll,...)` (rotation*4) | `Camera.rotation`/`pitch_internal` |
| `camera.distance/zoom` | `setCameraOrientation` `eyeLen` | `View.Zoom`→`zoom*2` | `Camera.zoom`/`distance1` |
| `camera.viewDist/clipNear/clipFar/baseX/baseY/width` | `Scene.setBounds` (`R/nb/Mb/Zb/Nb/vb`) | `scene.go` consts `viewDist/clipNear/clipFar`, `RenderTo(cx,cy)` | per-frame in obf `lb.a(...)`/`drawScene` |
| `window.plane` | `World.loadSection` plane arg | `View.Plane` | `client` current floor |
| `terrain.elevation` | `World.terrainHeight` (obf `L`) via `getTerrainHeight` ×3 | `Tile.GroundElevation`×3 (`terrain.go buildTerrain`) | `k.L[q][...]` |
| `terrain.groundColour` | `World.terrainColour` (obf `eb`) | `Tile.GroundTexture`→`terrainColours[]` | `k.eb` |
| `terrain.overlay` | `World.tileDecoration` (obf `R`), `getTileDecoration` | `Tile.GroundOverlay` (`ovl[][]`) | `k.R` |
| `terrain.roof` | `World.wallsRoof` (obf `A`), `getWallRoof` | `Tile.RoofTexture` | `k.A` |
| `terrain.wallH` | `World.wallsEastwest` (obf `P`) | `Tile.HorizontalWall` | `k.P` |
| `terrain.wallV` | `World.wallsNorthsouth` (obf `f`) | `Tile.VerticalWall` | `k.f` |
| `terrain.wallDiag` | `World.wallsDiagonal` (obf `s`) | `Tile.DiagonalWalls` | `k.s` |
| `scenery[]` | `World.addModels`/`loadMapData` `.loc` (diag +48000) | `facts.SceneryLoc`→`PlaceScenery` | `.loc` decode in `k` + obj arrays |
| `entities[]` | Mudclient actor arrays + `Scene.addSprite` | `View.Entities`→`DrawEntitySprites` | `client` npc/player arrays |
| `groundItems[]` | Mudclient ground-item arrays | `View.GroundItems`→`BuildGroundItems` | `client` ground-item arrays |
| `self.*` | Mudclient local player | `View.X/Y/Self*` | `client` localPlayer |
| `removedBoundaries[]` | live door-open state | `View.BoundaryRemoved` | `client` boundary updates |
| `models[].verts/faces` (L2) | `GameModel.vertexX/Y/Z`/`faceVertices`/`faceFillFront/Back`/`faceIntensity` (obf `a/ob/bc`,`o`,`V/qb`,`Hb`) | `GameModel.VertexX/Y/Z`, `FaceVertices`, `FaceFillFront/Back`, `FaceIntensity` (`model.go`) | `lb.Z[i]` → `ca.a/ob/bc/o/V/qb/Hb` |
| `models[].lightAmbience/Diffuse/Dir` | `GameModel` `Jb/Mb/g/Bb/Fb` | `GameModel.SetLight` (`lightAmbience` etc.) | `ca.Jb/Mb/g/Bb/Fb` |

---

## 5. The harness — render-from-dump + diff

### Per-engine "render a dump → PNG/int[]" entrypoints

**GO** — already 90% there. Add a thin `render.RenderDump(dump) ([]byte, []int32, error)`:
- Parse the schema; build a synthetic `pathfind.Landscape`-equivalent from `terrain.*` (a `gridLandscape` backed by the dumped arrays instead of map files), and a `View` from camera/window/entities/scenery/items.
- For L2: bypass `BuildTerrain`/etc. and load `models[]` directly into `Scene.Models` via `NewGameModel`/`AddVertex`/`AddFace`/`AddFixedFace`, set `Scene.Cam`, call `RenderTo`. Output `Surface.PNG()` and the raw `Surface.Pix`.

**DEOB Java** — add a `DumpRenderer` headless main (compiled against the deob `scene`/`world` packages):
- L1: construct `Surface(W,H)`, `Scene`, `World(scene,surface)`; **inject the dumped terrain grids directly into `World`'s `byte[][]` fields** (add a package-private `loadFromDump(...)` that fills `terrainHeight/terrainColour/tileDecoration/wallsRoof/wallsEastwest/wallsNorthsouth/wallsDiagonal/tileDirection`), bypassing `loadMapData`; then call `buildSection`/`addModels`. Pin the `Math.random()` speckle to the dumped `terrainSeed`.
- L2: feed `models[]` straight into `GameModel` instances and `Scene.addModel`.
- Camera via `setCameraOrientation`/`setBounds`, then `Scene.render(...)`.
- Grab `Surface.pixels` (obf `ua.rb`) int[] → write PNG with `ImageIO` (or emit raw int[] for the byte-exact diff).
- *Compile note (from prior findings):* `Scene`/`GameModel`/`Surface`/`World` are package-private/`final` and reference cross-class statics scattered on obf classes; the deob harness needs those static tables present. This is the known DEOB compile work and is the gating dependency for the DEOB engine (Mudclient itself is explicitly out of scope, but the `scene`+`world`+`data` packages must compile). Use `JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk`.

**JAR** — a rscplus `DumpRenderer` headless mode:
- L1/L2: load the dump, drive the obf client to the dumped state (replay-seek to the frame, OR inject grids the way the L1 hook reads them) and let the obf renderer run one `drawScene`.
- Grab `Renderer.pixels` (already the mirrored `ua.rb`) → PNG / raw int[].
- Run headless: `export JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk` (java.applet needs 17, not the PATH JDK 26); build via the portable Ant launcher per the environment notes.

### The diff
Two complementary diffs, in a small Go tool `cmd/renderdiff`:

1. **Pixel diff.** Decode the three PNGs (or compare raw int[] buffers when same-size). Emit:
   - per-pixel max channel delta, count of differing pixels, % differing,
   - a **diff heatmap PNG** (highlight pixels where |Δ|>threshold) per pair (GO↔JAR, DEOB↔JAR, GO↔DEOB),
   - a one-line verdict per pair with a tolerance (e.g. ≤1 LSB rounding tolerated; structural mismatch flagged).

2. **Structural diff ("is this face/wall present?").** Independent of rasterization quirks: from the L2 dump (or by instrumenting each builder to also emit its built model list), compare the *set of faces* each engine produced — keyed by (rounded centroid world-XYZ, vertex count, fill id). Reports faces present in JAR but missing in GO/DEOB (and extras). **This is what pinpoints the door/bridge/roof bugs**: a missing door quad, a bridge deck face with the wrong fill, a roof face that should be culled but isn't — surfaced as a structural delta rather than a fuzzy pixel cloud.

---

## 6. Phased build plan + the SINGLE first tool

### Phase 0 — schema + Go round-trip (no Java yet)
- Define `rscdump/1` (Go structs in `westworld/internal/rscdump` + JSON marshal).
- Add `render.RenderDump(dump)` (L1) reusing `RenderView` against a `gridLandscape` built from `terrain.*`.
- **THE FIRST TOOL** (cheapest path to a real 3-way diff): a **hand-authored single-tile dump** — one tile with one wall/door, flat terrain, fixed camera, no entities, RNG pinned to 0. This is a ~30-line JSON literal, no map files, no replay, no GameData ramps (emit raw colours). Render it through GO → PNG. This proves the dump→render path and gives the fixture every other engine targets.

### Phase 1 — DEOB engine
- Get `client.scene` + `client.world` + their static-table dependencies to compile under JDK 17 (the known gating work; Mudclient stays out of scope).
- Add `World.loadFromDump` + a `DumpRenderer` headless main → grab `Surface.pixels` → PNG/int[].
- Run the Phase-0 fixture through DEOB. **First 2-way diff: GO ↔ DEOB.** Cheap because both are our code and share constants we can align directly.

### Phase 2 — JAR engine (oracle) + the real 3-way diff
- rscplus `DumpRenderer`: load the Phase-0 fixture, inject the single-tile grid the way the L1 hook reads, run one `drawScene`, grab `Renderer.pixels` → PNG. (Hand-authored fixture avoids needing a replay for the first jar render.)
- Build `cmd/renderdiff` (pixel + structural). **First real 3-way diff on the single-tile-with-door scene.**

### Phase 3 — replay-driven real maps + the bug hunt
- Add the rscplus **L1 dump hook** reading `k`'s grids + `Camera.*` + actor arrays, driven by **replay seek** (deterministic source per §3b). Dump real Lumbridge/bridge/building frames.
- Run the 3-way diff over a fixture set chosen to hit each bug: a door tile (unwalkable door), a bridge-deck chunk seam (broken bridge), a roofed building from inside + outside (roof culling). Use the **structural diff** to localize each to a specific builder method, then fix GO/DEOB until pixel+structural diffs vs the JAR oracle go green.

### Phase 4 — L2 isolation + CI
- Add the L2 dump (jar reads built `lb.Z[]`; GO/DEOB load models directly) to bisect any remaining diff into builder-side vs rasterizer-side.
- Wire the green fixtures into a regression check (golden PNGs + structural sets) so future deob/Go edits can't silently regress faithfulness.

### First tool, stated plainly
**A hand-authored single-tile `rscdump/1` JSON (one wall/door, flat terrain, fixed camera, RNG pinned) + `render.RenderDump` to turn it into a PNG.** It is the cheapest artifact that (a) exercises the dump→render contract, (b) becomes the shared fixture all three engines render, and (c) is the seed for the first GO↔DEOB and then 3-way GO↔DEOB↔JAR diff — directly on the wall/door case where the unwalkable-door bug lives.

---

## Key file references
- DEOB: `reference/rsc-client/src/client/world/World.java` (`buildSection` L867-1103, `buildRoofs` L1165, `addModels` L792, `method422` L740, `getElevation` L391), `scene/Scene.java` (`setCameraOrientation` L444, `setBounds` L486, model list `Z` L131), `scene/GameModel.java` (verts/faces/`project` L1214), `scene/Surface.java` (`pixels` L104).
- GO: `render/render.go` (`View` L27, `RenderView` L130, `DrawEntitySprites` L352), `render/scene.go` (`Scene`/`RenderTo`/`rasterFace`), `render/camera.go` (`SetCamera`), `render/terrain.go` (bridge remap L107-120), `render/surface.go` (`Pix`/`PNG`), `render/model.go` (GameModel API), `cmd/cradle/spectate.go` (`buildLiveView` L183 — the existing L1-ish dump).
- JAR/rscplus: `src/Client/JClassPatcher.java` (`ua.rb→Renderer.pixels` hook L164, `ua.u/k→width/height` L162-163), `src/Game/Renderer.java` (`pixels` L85), `src/Game/Camera.java` (`rotation/zoom/lookat_x/y/distance*/pitch_internal` L31-58), `src/Game/Replay.java` (`frame_time_slice`/`timestamp` — the deterministic state source).
